title: Mandalorian heavy blaster

An improvement by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] upon standard [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] designs, the '''Mandalorian heavy blaster''' was a weapon in use during the latter [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Old Sith Wars]]. The [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Calo Nord]] used a pair of the [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], and examples of the weapon were available for purchase from [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]] [[Greeta Holda]] and Mandalorian [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]] [[Kex]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], respectively.

==Description==
The [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster]] was an improvement over the standard heavy [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] design. A major refinement made by its Mandalorian creators was that the weapon could fully accommodate aftermarket upgrades. The [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] dealt slightly more energy damage than the standard design, although it had a similar range.<ref name="TSL" /> Mandalorians claimed that the blaster was capable of crippling a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] with a single shot, but this assertion became widely known as an exaggeration. The blaster was [[Color/Legends|colored]] blue and gray while shots fired were red in color.<ref name="KOTOR" />

==History==
Created by the nomadic Mandalorians, the eponymous heavy blaster was in use<ref name="KOTOR" /> during the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] leading up to the conclusion of the [[Old Sith Wars]].<ref name="OSW">The ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' places the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' in 3956 BBY and the events of ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' in 3951 BBY, both near the end of the Old Sith Wars.</ref> The [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Calo Nord]] used a<ref name="KOTOR" /> modified<ref name="Uncivilized">{{SW|url=news/so-uncivilized-great-gunslingers-in-star-wars-part-1|series=So Uncivilized: Great Gunslingers in Star Wars|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> pair of the blasters when he [[Ambush on Tatooine (Jedi Civil War)|ambushed]] the then–[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]<ref name="KOTOR" /> in the year [[3956 BBY]].<ref name="OSW" /> However, Nord and his companions were [[Death/Legends|killed]] by Revan in the resulting fight. At the same [[Time/Legends|time]], in the [[City/Legends|town]] of [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]], [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] [[Merchant/Legends|storekeeper]] [[Greeta Holda]] retailed a Mandalorian heavy blaster for 2,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]].<ref name="KOTOR" /> [[3951 BBY|Five years later]],<ref name="OSW" /> another heavy blaster was sold by [[Kex]], a Mandalorian [[Quartermaster/Legends|quartermaster]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]], for the sum of 5,000 credits.<ref name="TSL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Mandalorian heavy blaster first appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', and appeared again in its [[2005]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. The blaster also received entries in the [[Prima Games]] strategy guides for [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|both]] [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|games]]. Due to the close-range combat system used in the games, the weapon had a maximum firing range of twenty-three [[Meter/Legends|meters]], which was the same as the games' other heavy blaster pistols.<ref name="TSLprima">[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> The attack modifier granted by the weapon is reduced between the two games, but no other game mechanic regarding the blaster changes.
